description: The Lo Russo clan was a Neapolitan Camorra clan operating in the Miano area of Naples, formed in the late 1970s by the Lo Russo brothers. It was a member of the Nuova Famiglia and later the Secondigliano Alliance, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and other crimes. The clan was considered overthrown after the arrests of its leaders and affiliates.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 15 April 2014, the new boss Antonio Lo Russo and his cousin Carlo Lo Russo were arrested by agents of National Gendarmerie Section de Recherches and by Carabinieri while quietly in a bar in Nice .
country_links.context: The Lo Russo clan's boss and his cousin were arrested in Nice, France, indicating the clan's presence there.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2017, assets worth 20 million euros belonging to the Potenza brothers, who are considered to be linked to the Lo Russo clan, were seized in Lugano , including bank accounts in BSI .
country_links.context: Assets linked to the Lo Russo clan were seized in Lugano, Switzerland, indicating the clan's financial footprint there.
